spec: Six contained edits, all inside existing sections — this page scored highest (8/9/8), so enhance only. (1) Page-local <style>: add .ts-block{scroll-margin-top:88px} so the hero's 'See the task breakdown →' anchor clears the 64px fixed nav. (2) After the .ts-table footnote (~line 429), insert one .quote-block (lime left-border blockquote + .quote-attr mono attribution) quoting an existing /results case study, followed by a mono .metric-how-style link 'See the full case study →' to /results#client-outcomes. Must stay inside the existing snap-sec. (3) Update three sprint card hrefs (~450/462/468) from the catalog to /solutions/sprints/seo-ppc, /solutions/sprints/event-followup, /solutions/sprints/content-brief. (4) Demote the builders CTA (~487) from .btn-primary to .btn-ghost (keep the margin-top:24px inline style). (5) Shorten the sprint-grid closing button (~476) to 'Get your free Readiness Score →' and move the routing promise into a 13px mono microcopy line beneath ('Answer 13 questions — we'll point you to the right sprint.'). (6) Wrap the guardrail section label (~498) in <span class="label-signal">. GUARDRAILS: no new sections, no snap-sec/hero/nav changes, no CTA additions.

(1) BUG: in .wm-header (~296-299) insert as the first child (empty corner cell) and scope all .wm-header span selectors to .wm-header > span (including :first-child transparent and :last-child border rules) so .wm-range stops inheriting padding/border — currently the readiness-axis labels render invisible/misaligned. (2) BUG: change .result-n values (~373/385) to counter-safe 'From $5k' and 'From $500' (no
, no ranges — animateCounter parses prefix+number+suffix); move ceilings into .result-nl captions: 'up to $15k · per sprint · 2–4 weeks' / 'up to $2,500/mo · cancel anytime'. (3) Move the entire #tiers-heading offer-stack section directly after the Readiness Ladder section (order-independent per reviewer: reveal observers and tier0 personalization use getElementById) — fixes products-referenced-before-defined AND breaks up the two adjacent identical lattice tables. (4) Hero (~225): add .hero-actions — .btn-primary 'Get your free Readiness Score →' (/tools/readiness-score, the default href so personalization can rewrite it) + .btn-ghost 'See the offer stack →' (#tiers-heading). (5) Normalize .result-tag rungs to 'Tier 0 · Free' / 'Tier 1 · Start here' / 'Tier 2 · Score 50+' / 'Tier 3 · Score 70+' and add a Training rung below the stack as the left-signal-accent callout (1px border + 4px lime left border): 'Score under 50? Start with Training →' → /training. (6) Delete the .ai-advantage-note block; end the manifesto's second .manifesto-body with its one novel sentence. (7) Mobile: add mono micro-labels ('Low readiness · 0–69' / 'High readiness · 70–100') inside each .wm-cell, hidden ≥700px, shown in the <700px query, so the matrix axis survives the stack.
(1) Page-local

(1) Add a compact pattern-B .page-hero above the catalog section: visible h1 with .blink-dot (replace the sr-only h1 at line 217), pulsing-dot eyebrow '— Sprint catalog', the .cat-tracks-intro copy promoted to p.lead inside the hero, and a .page-meta chip row ('15 sprints · 5 programs · 6 guides', 'Fixed scope · 1–4 weeks'). Safe relative to main.js: the score banner injects immediately before .cat-filter-wrap, which stays where it is. (2) FUNNEL-CRITICAL: append ?sprints=<id> to all nine 'Request this sprint →' card CTAs (ids must match request-proposals' SPRINT_META_PAGE — pairs with that page's spec). (3) Add a one-line mono legend under the filter tabs styled like .cat-filter-label: '● Runs on exports · ● Needs a documented process · ● Needs live API access'; replace the hardcoded #FF4444 red dot with a 1px-bordered mist dot (red stays reserved for destructive states). (4) Port the .card hover onto .cat-card: transform:translateY(-3px) + a ::before 2px signal bar with scaleX(0→1) sweep (copy .card::before verbatim) + translateX nudge on .cat-cta, all inside the same ≥900px hover guard. (5) Closing section (~692): add .section-label ('Next step' with .label-signal), keep both existing buttons, and add a mono tertiary link 'or get your free Readiness Score →' (/tools/readiness-score) styled like .footer-cta-link beneath them — the copy already promises it. (6) Add min-height:44px (align-items:center) to .cat-tab and .add-proposal-btn. The single aria-pressed line in the tab click handler is the only JS touch — defer it to the manual pass if agent-run. GUARDRAILS: do not alter filter logic, the ca_plan tray mechanics, or the banner injection point.

The funnel terminus — all JS/form work. (1) Add a hidden <form name="proposal-request" data-netlify="true"> registering every payload field (sprints, goal, timeline, budget, team, name, email, company, score); in submitProposal(), AJAX-POST it (copy the audit page's working pattern, ~lines 455-474) before showing the confirmation, with a mailto fallback on failure. Currently the payload is only console.log()'d (line 469). (2) Add the five found-* Foundation sprints to SPRINT_META_PAGE (line 340); verify all ids against the catalog's SPRINT_ID_MAP so URL pre-fill and the add panel cover the full catalog. (3) In init() (~428), when no ?sprints= param, seed `selected` from the ca_plan localStorage cart (filter to SPRINT_META_PAGE keys). (4) In renderPills(), keep #pr-sprint-list visible when selection is empty: render the dashed 'No sprints selected yet' note as a row above the .pr-add-sprint button, relabelled '+ Add a sprint' at count 0 — never hide the page's only picker. (5) Validation: add an aria-live="polite" mono error line above .pr-submit-btn with per-failure messages ('Add your name to continue', 'Select at least one sprint'); scrollIntoView({block:'center',behavior:'smooth'}) before focus(), especially for the empty-selection branch whose message currently appears off-viewport. (6) Move .breadcrumb out of .pr-wrap to first child of <main id="main-content"> (audit-page placement); give .pr-wrap padding-top:16px. (7) Visual: give .pr-hero the .page-hero dotted-grid ::before + signal seam, lift the h1 clamp toward clamp(32px,5vw,52px), add .reveal reveal-delay-1/2/3 to the three .pr-section blocks. (8) Add the audit-style 4-up checkmark trust strip between hero and form ('3 proposals guaranteed · Delivered in 5 business days · Every specialist vetted · No obligation'); keep .pr-guarantee at the bottom as the pre-commit close. (9) Confirmation state: promote 'Sprint prep resources →' to .btn-primary, keep 'Browse more sprints →' as .btn-ghost.

All changes are in the page script / injected templates. (1) In .score-reveal, add a .btn-primary anchor 'See your recommended next step ↓' (#intake-gate) directly under .score-tagline; reorder showScorecard() so the .intake-gate block renders BEFORE ${renderShareResults()}. (2) Render the unused .score-cta block (styles exist ~line 132) in the scorecard AND repeat both links in the intake-sent confirmation: primary 'Build your sprint plan →' /get-started, secondary 'Book the Audit →' /solutions/campaign-audit — the success state is currently text-only with zero links. (3) Count .score-number from 0 to finalScore over ~1200ms ease-out cubic (replicate main.js's counter), guarded by the page's REDUCED_MOTION const (set textContent immediately when true); stagger .tier-badge and .score-tagline with .15s/.3s animation-delays so the reveal reads score → tier → verdict. (4) Scroll reveals for the injected report: replicate main.js's one-shot IntersectionObserver in the page script (threshold .08, rootMargin '0px 0px -40px 0px', unobserve after first fire), adding .reveal to each brd-block/dim-grid/roadmap/guardrails/share-block AFTER injection, with .is-visible applied immediately under REDUCED_MOTION. Do NOT just swap animate-in for .reveal — main.js only observes nodes present at load. (5) Restyle .constraint-box as the sanctioned left-signal callout (border:1px solid var(--border); border-left:4px solid var(--signal)) keeping .constraint-label as the mono lime eyebrow; replace the emoji .mf-icons with mono uppercase chips (RISK / DATA / CONTROL / SOLO / TOOLS); keep #FFE200 for pure text warnings only. (6) Wrap roadmap, guardrails, and the weight-sim in .brd-block containers with .brd-eyebrow labels; add an h2 to the score reveal (sr-only or eyebrow-styled) so the outline survives the state swap. (7) submitIntake(): aria-live='polite' error line under the submit button (reuse .intake-note styles, color #FFE200) + aria-invalid and border tint on the failing field — validation is currently silent. (8) Hero polish: page-hero dotted-grid ::before on #rs-intro, .blink-dot on the h1, and the missing inside the .signal-badge. GUARDRAIL: do not touch the question flow, scoring math, tier thresholds, weight-simulator mechanics, or the Netlify intake wiring.

State-machine page — main loop only. (1) Replace the phantom animate-in class (lines ~269, 291, 297, 316, 324 — defined nowhere, verified) with reveal (+ reveal-delay-1/2 on successive blocks); main.js handles observation and reduced-motion. (2) FUNNEL: in the unscored state, if ca_plan has items, render the saved sprints with a 'Your plan is saved — take the 4-minute score to unlock proposals' banner (or offer direct continuation to /request-proposals?sprints=…) instead of silently discarding the cart. (3) In renderBuilders() (~594), replace each card's immediate 'Request builder →' exit with the existing .gs-add-btn '+ Add to plan' toggle (togglePlanItem/PLAN, type:'builder') so builders join .gs-plan-tray and #gs-plan-cta stays the single forward CTA; keep 'View profile →' as a secondary. (4) Append a closing lime-outline CTA box after #gs-builder-grid (signal-badge + h3.display + p + .btn-primary mirroring #gs-plan-cta's href) so the scored state has a forward exit at the end of the read. (5) Unscored hero: add inside the 'Step 1 of 3' signal-badge, give .gs-hero the .page-hero dotted-grid ::before, raise the h1 clamp toward page-hero scale, and convert the '13 questions · 4 minutes · No login' line into a .page-meta chip row. (6) Rebuild .gs-steps as the homepage
    of .step rows (72px ghost .step-n), keeping the .step-tag mono chips. (7) Swap .gs-section-label for the shared .section-label (+ .label-signal) and add an h3.display under each ('Recommended for you', 'Choose a builder'). (8) Move .breadcrumb out of .gs-wrap to sit directly under
    .

Shared chrome — main loop only, test scored + unscored states on home, catalog, and get-started after each change. (1) In updateNavFunnel() (~335-448), drive the nav CTA from the already-computed ctaCfg (FUNNEL.cta defines label+href per stage) instead of the hardcoded binary that always sends scored visitors to the catalog. (2) Route the 'proposals' stage through /get-started (where the plan tray lives) — or add Request Proposals to the main nav — so the nav-next-step highlight can actually render; same consideration for the 'prep' stage (/resources/sprint-prep). (3) Delete the dead non-mega Solutions-dropdown personalization block (~lines 369-437): it early-returns on .mega menus, which every page uses, so it can never execute (~70 dead lines). GUARDRAIL: no other changes — the counter parser, .reveal observer, typewriter, snap-indicator builder, and reduced-motion guards are contracts the whole site depends on.

(1) Replace the inline-styled hero CTA div with
; add a .page-meta chip row ('White-label', 'Per-client guardrails', 'Your brand out front') and a .blink-dot on the h1. (2) Add a .hero-metrics strip under the hero paragraph — three .metric blocks (accounts-per-AM lift, hours returned per client, margin protected) with .metric-n/.metric-l; main.js animates it free. The page currently contains zero numbers. (3) Open all four sections with .section-label + .label-signal. (4) Rebuild the four .aud-pains as .kw-row rows in a bordered wrapper (kw-term = pain, kw-vol = mono cost tag 'MARGIN' / 'HOURS' / 'RISK'). (5) 'What you get': put real mono-lime values in the .kw-vol slots (replace the decorative ✦), and promote the bare inline-styled guardrails anchor into the left-signal callout box: strong 'Every client is isolated' + one sentence + mono lime 'How the guardrails work →'. (6) FUNNEL: point both 'Talk partnerships →' CTAs (lines 142, 182) at mailto:hello@campaignautomation.ai?subject=White-label%20partnership — the current target requires picking sprints and (until fixed) doesn't submit. (7) Close: add a .tagline-footer section ('Your brand out front. Our machines underneath.' + the partnerships CTA) and drop .bone from the CTA section so the signal-outline box glows on dark.

(1) Replace the inline-styled hero CTA div (~line 141) with <div class="hero-actions"> — this silently re-enables main.js tier personalization (it targets .hero-actions .btn-primary and this href IS a default destination) and the <640px full-width stacking. (2) Add a .hero-metrics strip under the hero paragraph (three .metric blocks, homepage markup verbatim — counters animate automatically); append a .blink-dot to the h1. (3) Open all four sections with .section-label + .label-signal ('The symptoms', 'The method', 'The handover', 'Free tool'). (4) 'Sound familiar?': rebuild the four pains as .kw-row rows in a bordered wrapper (kw-term = pain, kw-vol = ✕), plus one p.lead ('If two of these sound like your Tuesday, keep reading.'). (5) Upgrade the bare 'How we measure the impact →' link into the left-signal callout box (1px border + 4px lime left border + mono lime link). (6) Closing box: add the missing .signal-badge 'Free tool' eyebrow (with dot span) and make the h3 persona-specific ('Find the workflow burning your team — in four minutes.') so it stops duplicating the .footer-cta-h verbatim one scroll below. (7) Bone cards: add .card-tag labels ('01 · Diagnose' … '04 · Handover') and manual reveal reveal-delay-1..4 (grid-2 gets no auto-stagger).

(1) Open all three sections with .section-label + .label-signal. (2) Hero: replace the inline-styled CTA div with .hero-actions; append the .blink-dot to the h1; add a .page-meta chip row ('Auditable by design', 'Reversible actions', 'Board-ready reporting'). (3) Add the .hero-metrics proof strip (13 hrs / 27% ROAS / +2 QS) — executives are the audience most likely to check for evidence, and the page has zero numbers. (4) SHOWPIECE: turn the static 'What you get' terminal into a typed agent log — add data-animate to .terminal, replace the dead .kw-rows with [data-type] lines using .t-comment/.t-green/.t-dim roles plus a trailing .cursor: 'trigger: CPA breach detected' → 'action: paused 3 ad sets (within approved bounds)' → 'logged: full audit trail written' → 'impact: −$4,120 wasted spend / 30 days', ending with the four deliverables as t-green checklist lines. main.js supplies typewriter, aria-live mirror, and reduced-motion skip. (5) Pains: add a p.lead and reframe item 3 as a pain ('ROI reports you couldn't defend to a CFO — vanity dashboards, not verified numbers.'). (6) Closing box: add .signal-badge 'Free · 4 minutes · No login' above the h3 and a mono secondary 'or go straight to the Audit →' (/solutions/campaign-audit) under the btn-primary. (7) Replace the bare 'See the outcomes →' anchor with <a href="/results" class="btn-ghost btn-sm">; add .card-tag + reveal-delay-1..4 to the four bone cards.

(1) Swap the page-local .rs-metrics block (lines ~191-207 + local CSS ~57-78) for the homepage's .hero-metrics > .metric markup — .metric-n keeping the exact '13 hrs' prefix/number/suffix textContent format + aria-labels the counter parser expects, .metric-l captions, .metric-how proof links. Delete the local .rs-* CSS. The proof page's headline numbers then count up via main.js with no JS changes. (2) Animate the TAI terminal (~294-300): .terminal[data-animate] containing .scanline + per-line [data-type] divs (.t-dim for the comment line, .t-green/default for trigger/action/guardrail/impact) + trailing .cursor. (3) Restructure the lopsided bone grid-2: TAI prose left, the newly animated terminal right, 'Read the full TAI framework →' .btn-ghost directly under the prose. (4) FUNNEL: in #client-outcomes, link each .result-tag/card to its /solutions/sprints/* detail page and add a centered .btn-ghost 'See the sprints behind these results →' (/solutions/automation-sprints) after the fourth card — secondary affordance only; the score primary stays at .tagline-footer. (5) Add 'Start with the Audit →' (/solutions/campaign-audit) as a secondary beside the closing score CTA so proof converts forward. (6) Hero: add .page-meta chips ('Medians across engagements', '4 case results', 'TAI-logged'). (7) Relabel the ROAS/QS .metric-how links to match their #time-saved destination ('See which tasks drive it →').

(1) CONTRAST BLOCK (accessibility, do first): in the page <style> add .section.bone .pillar-tag.signal{color:#333;border-color:var(--signal);background:rgba(200,241,53,.18)} (the page's own .tai-node.accent treatment), .sprint-callout-label{color:#333}, restyle the inline lime Portal link (line 341) as a dotted-underline ink .gloss-link, and darken .tai-sidebar nav a to #555 — lime-on-bone is currently ~1.2:1 and the page's signposts, both callout labels, and the cross-sell link are functionally invisible. (2) Remove .snap-sec from all three sections (lines 199/205/395) — main.js suppresses the indicator below 2 snap-secs automatically; verify no stuck dot rail at ≥900px. Fill the recovered hero with .page-meta chips ('5 stages · Ships with every sprint · Exportable CSV/JSON') and a .blink-dot on the h1. (3) Move the #guardrails section between #trigger and #action to match the loop diagram's stage order (pure cut-and-paste); reorder the two sidebar links to match. (4) Activate free scrollspy: add class pillar-nav to the sidebar's inner <nav> and class article-section to each .tai-article section (ids already exist) — main.js's existing bindings supply scrollspy + smooth scroll; keep the .tai-sidebar bone/sticky overrides. (5) Add .reveal to each article section and both .sprint-callouts; optionally reveal-delay-1..5 on the five .tai-node spans so the loop assembles left-to-right. (6) Animate ONLY the climactic activity-log terminal (~line 383): data-animate + .t-comment header + .t-dim/.t-green roles + .cursor + .scanline; leave the other four static for scannability. (7) Single-primary rule: swap both mid-article callout .btn-primary.btn-sm to .btn-ghost.btn-sm and add the bone override .sprint-callout .btn-ghost{color:var(--ink)}.

(1) HEADLINE MOVE: extract the Readiness Score card out of the first grid and rebuild it as the homepage's slate free-tool section between the library grid and the topics grid — .section with inline background:var(--slate), .signal-badge 'Free tool' eyebrow, h2.display, p.lead, .btn-primary → /tools/readiness-score, paired bordered .kw-row 'What you'll learn' checklist. The remaining 4 cards form a clean 2×2 and the orphaned fifth-card row disappears. (2) Open the section properly: .section-label with .label-signal ('The library') + h2.display ('Pick where you want to begin.') + one-line p.lead; replace the inline-styled 'Browse by topic' divider with a real .section-label. (3) Split 'Browse by topic' into its own .section.bone using .bone-card cards (.card-tag 'Topic', h4, p, .card-arrow inside .card-link) in a .grid-4 with data-stagger — page rhythm becomes dark → slate → bone → tagline. (4) Hero: add .page-meta chips ('104 articles', '355 glossary terms', 'Free 4-min score', 'Templates & prompt packs') + .blink-dot on the h1. (5) Remove the inert data-stagger attributes from the .grid-2s and assign sequential reveal-delay classes in DOM order (row 2 currently reveals right-before-left); the new grid-4 staggers automatically. (6) Add .url mono meta lines to the remaining pillar-cards ('104 guides →', '4 min · no login', '355 terms', 'Free downloads').

(1) Hero: change the eyebrow to 'Resources · The library' (it currently duplicates the h1 verbatim), add the .blink-dot to the h1, and add a .page-meta chip row ('104 articles', '8 topics', 'Filterable & searchable') — or, better, a .hero-metrics strip (104 / 8 / 355) so main.js counts up the library's scale on arrival. (2) Add the existing .rec-badge chip ('Start here') to the four featured cards and split the featured block into its own .section with inline background:var(--slate) so the recommendation visually outranks the 100-card wall. (3) Add .reveal to .art-feat-grid, to the .art-bar+.art-gallery section, and to the .tagline-footer; do NOT put data-stagger on the 104-card gallery. (4) COPY BUG: strip the trailing ' | Campaign Automation AI' from the four card h3s (lines ~180, 188, 202, 249) and their mirrored data-text attributes; note for the owner to patch the card generator so the suffix is removed at emission. (5) Convert both group labels to <h2 class="art-section-label"> (existing local styles apply) and wrap 'Start here' in .label-signal — restores the h1→h2→h3 outline. (6) Add role="status" aria-live="polite" to #artcount — attribute only, no script change; the existing innerHTML update then announces filter results. (7) OPTIONAL, defer to manual pass: a 'Clear filters ×' .art-chip button inside #art-empty that reactivates the All chip, empties #artq, and calls the existing apply().

(1) Swap the page-local .tr-grid > .training-card catalog (CSS lines 25-39, markup 153-197) for the shared .training-grid > .training-item component that already exists in css/style.css:754 — convert .training-tag to .t-tag, keep the .duration line and existing .reveal/.reveal-delay-* classes per tile, delete the local .tr-grid/.training-card/.training-tag CSS. This fixes the off-brand light-on-dark cards AND the cramped 2-col-at-400px mobile bug via the shared 900px collapse. (2) FUNNEL: directly under the grid add the left-signal callout box (1px border + 4px lime left border): strong 'Not sure which program fits your team?' + one sentence + mono lime link to /tools/readiness-score, plus a secondary mono line 'or email hello@campaignautomation.ai about team training →' — the catalog is currently a dead end. (3) Proof beat in the bone section: a .quote-block from a past cohort attendee, or minimally one line with a mono lime link 'Every program teaches the TAI framework we run in client accounts →' (/results/trigger-action-impact). (4) Remove .snap-sec from ALL sections (partial removal would still spawn the indicator at 2+); keep one .reveal per section; verify no .snap-indicator at ≥900px. (5) Hero: .page-meta chips ('6 programs', 'Half-day → 3 days', 'Online or in-person', 'Certificate available') + .blink-dot on the h1. (6) Bone section: wrap the label in .label-signal, switch ✦ bullets to →, drop the inline #555/#444 colors in favor of the .section.bone overrides.

(1) Motion: change each
to
; add reveal reveal-delay-1..4 to the four .bone-cards (grid-2 gets no auto-stagger); mirror the homepage closer with .reveal on the tagline-footer

and reveal-delay-2 on its .btn-primary. (2) Rebuild 'By the numbers' as a .hero-metrics strip under the page-hero — each stat a .metric with .metric-n (keep the prefix+number+suffix textContent + aria-label format), .metric-l caption, and a .metric-how link to /results#client-outcomes; the trust page's numbers then animate and link to evidence. (3) Hand-offs: wrap Belief 2's .bone-card in .card-link → /results/trigger-action-impact and Belief 3's → /training, adding .card-arrow to each; add one mono lime inline link in the heritage section ('See the framework →'). (4) People beat: insert one short dark section before the tagline-footer reusing the homepage builders pattern — .section-label 'The people', grid-2 with h2.display + p.lead left and the .bld-av monogram avatar cluster right, closed with .btn-ghost 'Meet the builders →' (/builders). (1) REPAIR THE HERO (lines 136-142): strip the tagline-footer class from the subtitle (a plain

gets the correct .page-hero p treatment — the section-level class currently injects 80px padding, centering, and a radial glow into the first screen); wrap CTAs in .hero-actions (.btn-primary 'Request portal access →' + .btn-ghost 'See pricing →' #pricing); replace the .color-shift strobe with a static style="color:var(--signal)" span on just 'intelligence layer'; move 'Early access · Invite only' from the second .signal-badge into a .page-meta chip row so the hero has one eyebrow. (2) Add .section-label (+ .label-signal) to all seven sections and class="lead" on intro paragraphs. (3) Roadmap: put 01-04 in .step-n (the 44px component overflows on 'M1-3'…'M12-18') and surface month ranges as mono .card-tag-style micro-labels above each h3. (4) Pricing: tier names → h3; add display:flex;flex-direction:column to .pricing-tier so the margin-top:auto buttons align; .signal-badge 'Most popular' on Growth; data-stagger on the grid-3; id="pricing" on the section. (5) After 'What the portal does not do', add the left-signal callout: strong 'Not sure you're ready for the portal?' + mono lime 'Start with the Automation Audit →' (/solutions/campaign-audit) — the four idea sections currently contain zero links. (6) Convert 'What the portal does not do' to a dark section of .kw-rows (the homepage candor pattern), reserving bone for the screens showcase and pricing peaks. (7) Screens: grid-2 → .grid-4 with data-stagger + .card-tag numbering ('01 · Dashboard' … '08 · Reports'); in the data-model terminal use .kw-term/.kw-intent classes instead of inline styles.

(1) Swap the hand-rolled .bld-stats strip for .hero-metrics > .metric (.metric-n with the prefix+number+suffix textContent format the parser expects — keep the lime 'wk' span since .metric-n span is already signal-styled; .metric-l captions); delete the local .bld-stats/.bld-stat CSS. The 15 / 7 / 2-4wk / 100% stats then count up on load. (2) Add class="reveal" to the three <section class="section"> wrappers and the .tagline-footer (this is the only funnel page with zero scroll motion); do NOT put data-stagger on .bld-grid (custom auto-fill grid, not covered); optional reveal-delay-1 on the founder card. (3) FLOW FIX: retarget the tagline-footer .btn-primary to #join with 'Apply to join →' so the recruiting close matches 'Come build with us.' (append the .blink-dot too) — the client funnel is preserved by the .footer-cta band immediately below, which keeps the free-score CTA. (4) Founder section → .grid-2: h2.display ('Founded by a builder.') + one-sentence p.lead left, the existing .bld-card.bld-founder right — fixes the h1→h3 heading gap and the dead right half. (5) Card hover: .bld-card{transition:transform .2s,border-color .2s} + :hover{transform:translateY(-3px);border-color:var(--signal-dim)}; keep the existing <900px hover neutralizer. (6) TRUST: change the .bld-badge on illustrative cards to 'Open role · illustrative' so the disclosure travels with the card instead of living only in the scrolled-away disclaimer. (7) Add required to the #bf-spec select — the data-spec/?specialty= prefill already fills it for card-driven applicants.

(1) STRUCTURAL PASS: split the single wrapper
(line 173) into four .section blocks — guardrails / agency / go-deeper / library — each opening with .section-label + .label-signal ('Control layer', 'For agencies', 'Go deeper', 'From the library'); make the agency section .section.bone and swap its two .card elements to .bone-card for the page's one inversion beat. — it shows agent config, exactly what the typewriter motif dramatizes.
